title: Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544 (seventy-six-documentary-group selection)
summary: The checked-in LinDDoc XML is a repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic text derived from selected matter in Andrew Clark's 1914 EETS volume Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544. Clark's volume combines sixty-three documentary groups selected by F. J. Furnivall and transcribed by an unnamed copyist with thirteen additional groups transcribed by C. W. Foster; Foster also collated a multitude of doubtful places in the original transcripts against the manuscript sources. Clark explicitly says he never inspected the registers, so the material transcripts and Foster collation are modeled between the edition and eight physical witness groups now held by Lincolnshire Archives. The U-M encoding retains the printed title page and document sequence on pp. 37–270 but omits substantial edition apparatus. A complete scan of the 1914 edition is public and cached. No publicly accessible complete manuscript facsimile was found for the registers or consistory books; current catalog, onsite microfilm, commercial microfilm, enquiry, and paid reproduction routes are recorded separately.
review_notes: The primary cataloging unit is Clark's seventy-six-documentary-group edited selection, not one medieval work, one institutional author, or one physical witness.
Historical vernacular is split between Middle English and sixteenth-century English; modern editorial English and Latin are separate layers.
Clark did not inspect the registers. Direct edition-to-register transcription edges would erase the unnamed copyist's and Foster's material intermediaries and are therefore not used.
DIOC/REG/19 is a composite register whose selected ff. 41r and 43r–46v belong to the 1450–1452 sede-vacante section, not to Marmaduke Lumley's own episcopal section despite Clark's source label.

Appendix IX is mapped with high confidence, but still inferentially, to DIOC/REG/24 rather than DIOC/REG/23; archive confirmation remains necessary. Main LX is securely mapped to LCC WILLS/1541, while the current item-level reference for main XXIII remains unresolved beyond the LCC WILLS series.

Current U-M public-domain language, the pinned XML's restrictive legacy availability statement, the repository's software-worded Unlicense, archive-image permission terms, and commercial microfilm terms are recorded as separate layer-specific claims.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for LinDDoc
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow, supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 526,278 bytes at the recorded CME submodule commit.
Its DLPSTEXTCLASS BODY contains two DIV1, 76 DIV2, 156 DIV3, 1,485 P, 234 PB, 156 MILESTONE, and two L elements.

The header declares only Latin. Direct inspection establishes Middle English and sixteenth-century English as historical vernacular layers, Latin as a secondary documentary language, and modern English as the editorial language.

The pinned file is structurally and bytewise distinct from the current public raw XML download, which uses an ETS/EEBO wrapper, removes legacy NODE attributes, and embeds a newer availability statement.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text LinDDoc
entities.description: A manually keyed and page-oriented electronic representation of selected content in Clark's 1914 edition, now presented through the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
notes: The editorial declaration says Sencor keyboarded the text; five percent was proofed and found within one error in 20,000 characters; Janet Erickson encoded and proofread it; Kenneth Church reviewed the encoding.
The encoding retains the printed title page, the sixty-three main groups, appendix introductions, and thirteen additional groups, corresponding to printed pp. 37–270.
It explicitly omits indentation, contents, main forewords, indexes, and textual notes. Direct edition comparison also shows that the Prefatory Note, Grammar Notes, and Additional Notes are absent.
The current downloadable XML is a transformed representation of the same identified digital item, not a byte-identical copy of the pinned repository artifact.

entities.label: Andrew Clark's 1914 Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544
alternate_labels: Early English Text Society, Original Series 149
entities.description: The immediate printed source of the electronic text and the first publication of the seventy-six-documentary-group edited selection, including substantial linguistic and historical apparatus omitted from the encoding.

identifiers.scheme: Early English Text Society Original Series

bibliographic.citation: Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544, edited, with notes and indexes, by Andrew Clark, Early English Text Society, Original Series 149 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., Ltd., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1914).
bibliographic.title: Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 149
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., Ltd.; Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press

notes: Clark's Prefatory Note explicitly calls the volume's contents seventy-six pre-Reformation documents, but each number is modeled as a documentary group rather than presumed to be one independent physical instrument.

The full volume contains preliminaries; Forewords pp. 1–36; main documents I–LXIII pp. 37–242; appendix introductions and documents I–XIII pp. 243–270; Grammar Notes pp. 271–341; Additional Notes pp. 342–344; and four indexes pp. 345–382.
Clark states that he never inspected the registers. The edition's historical text is mediated through the Furnivall-era transcripts, Foster's supplied copies, and Foster's collation.

entities.label: Unnamed copyist's working transcripts of Furnivall's sixty-three selected groups
entities.description: The material transcript bundle commissioned after Furnivall personally inspected the episcopal registers and selected characteristic English or quasi-English documents. Furnivall annotated and chronologically arranged the transcripts before Clark edited them.
notes: Provisionally typed catalog_description because schema 1.0.0 has no historical working-transcript entity type; this is a material editorial intermediary, not a medieval manuscript witness.
Clark considered the unnamed copyist's work careful and intelligent but relied on Foster's manuscript collation for a multitude of doubtful places.
Part of the John Asserby inventory transcript had been lost from the EETS bundle and was replaced by Foster; the exact lost span is not identified.

entities.label: C. W. Foster's collation and corrections for the sixty-three main groups
entities.description: The otherwise untraced record of Foster's checks against the manuscript sources in a multitude of doubtful places, together with corrections and archival information supplied to Clark.
notes: Provisionally typed catalog_description because the schema lacks a working-collation entity type.
The broad editorial statement proves collation of many doubtful passages but does not identify every corrected reading or certify a full line-by-line collation of all sixty-three groups.

entities.label: C. W. Foster's supplied transcripts for thirteen additional groups and the John Asserby replacement
entities.description: Foster's accurate copies of Appendix I–XIII and his replacement for the portion of main document XXIII that had disappeared from the older EETS transcript bundle.
notes: Provisionally typed catalog_description because the schema lacks a working-transcript type; this intermediary is not a second archival witness.
Clark first introduces five Foster transcripts at p. 243 and then credits Foster's knowledge and transcription of eight further documents in the Additional Note at p. 254.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives DIOC/REG/19, composite episcopal register
entities.description: A composite episcopal register now cataloged as DIOC/REG/19. Clark selected main groups II–III from ff. 43r–46v and 41r; the current record places these leaves in the December 1450–May 1452 sede-vacante section rather than Lumley's own episcopal section.

date_statements.label: selected entries
date_statements.notes: The dates describe the selected sede-vacante entries, not every component of the composite register.

holding.notes: Current official record; onsite access is normally through archive microfilm MF 6/18.

notes: The exact codicological boundaries and full physical extent require inspection of the current catalog and complete surrogate.
Main II, Thomas Cumberworth will: ff. 43r–46v; main III, Robert Sutton will: f. 41r.
Clark calls this Lumley's register and notes that it remained in use after his death; the current section-level catalog evidence makes the selected leaves sede-vacante records.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives DIOC/REG/20, John Chedworth episcopal register
entities.description: The episcopal register from which the unnamed copyist transcribed main groups I and IV–XX and Foster transcribed Appendix I–II.

date_statements.label: Chedworth register and selected entries
date_statements.notes: The earliest selected will is dated 1450; Chedworth's episcopate began later, so documentary date and register compilation must not be conflated.

holding.notes: Current official record; onsite access is normally through archive microfilm MF 6/18.

notes: Main I uses ff. 55r–56r, with a second copy at ff. 84v–85v collated in the edition; main IV–XX use ff. 12v–14v, 20v–26r, 35v–38v, 43v, 46v–50r, 70r–72v, 91r, and 240r, discontinuously.
Main XII is attributed only apparently to f. 14r or 14v and is explicitly described as presumably incomplete.
Appendix I–II use f. 7r and ff. 18v–19r.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives DIOC/REG/22, John Russell episcopal register
entities.description: The Russell register from which Foster transcribed Appendix VI–VIII.

holding.notes: Current official record; onsite access is normally through archive microfilm MF 6/19.

notes: Appendix VI, the 1485 statute: f. 77r; Appendix VII, the Newark bridge contract: ff. 77r–78r; Appendix VIII, John Goodwin's will and codicil: ff. 87r–87v.

entities.label: William Smith episcopal register containing Appendix IX, probably DIOC/REG/24
entities.description: The William Smith register volume containing the commission printed as Appendix IX at f. 87r–v. The source-note genre and current register descriptions make DIOC/REG/24 a high-confidence inferred match: DIOC/REG/23 is the 408-folio institution-type volume, while DIOC/REG/24 is the 335-folio miscellaneous and memoranda-type volume. Clark names only Smith's Register, so archive confirmation is still required before treating the shelfmark as certain.

holding.notes: Held at Lincolnshire Archives. DIOC/REG/24 is a high-confidence inference, not a confirmed archival crosswalk; the exact shelfmark remains null pending archive confirmation.

physical_description.format: Episcopal register; probably the miscellaneous and memoranda volume DIOC/REG/24

notes: Appendix IX prints the commission to the Abbot of Peterborough from f. 87r–v.

DIOC/REG/24 is the high-confidence candidate because the Appendix IX commission is administrative or memoranda material at f. 87, while DIOC/REG/23 is the institution-type volume. This remains an inference pending archive confirmation.
The residual volume uncertainty is physical, not textual: it must not be represented as one aggregate codex comprising DIOC/REG/23 and DIOC/REG/24.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives DIOC/REG/25, Wolsey and William Atwater episcopal register
entities.description: The register from which Foster transcribed Appendix X–XIII.

holding.notes: Current official record; the archive guide routes DIOC/REG/25 through microfilm MF 6/22.

notes: Wolsey material occupies ff. 1–11v, Atwater material ff. 12–134, and sede-vacante material ff. 135–137.
Appendix X–XIII use ff. 93r–93v, 92v–93r, 83r–83v, and 95r–95v respectively.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives DIOC/REG/26, John Longland Register of Memoranda
alternate_labels: Bishop John Longland's Register of Memoranda
entities.description: The Memoranda portion of Longland's register, source of main groups XXI–XXII, XXIV–LIX, and LXI–LXIII and Appendix III–V. Clark distinguishes it from the Institutions portion and says all Longland documents selected for the volume came from the Memoranda.

date_statements.label: register coverage
date_statements.notes: One selected enrolled agreement dates from 1509; the act's date does not redefine the later register's physical date.

historical_designations: Bishop John Longland's Register of Memoranda
holding.notes: Current official record; the archive record routes DIOC/REG/26 through local microfilm MF 6/22, while the commercial Gale guide lists Episcopal Register XXVI on reel 17.

notes: The complete folio range through f. 310 supports Clark's selected loci through f. 300. DIOC/REG/27's Longland portion ends at f. 274v and therefore cannot contain Clark's later Longland loci.

Main XXI–XXII use ff. 240r and 242v–243r; main XXIV–XXXVIII use ff. 231r, 232v–235r, 238r–244r, 248r–250r, and 263v–264r, discontinuously. For main XXXI, Clark prints f. 238v; the XML's 283v is a transposition.
Main XXXIX–LIX use ff. 254v–286v, discontinuously; main LXI uses ff. 292v–293v; main LXII uses ff. 299r–299v.
For main LVII, Clark prints the second milestone as f. 278r; the XML's conflicting value should not replace the edition locator.

For main LXIII, Clark's caption says ff. 229v–230v, but the internal printed folio marks are ff. 299v, 300r, and 300v and the chronology supports ff. 299v–300v. The inherited conflict is preserved pending direct witness inspection.
Appendix III–V use ff. 227v–228r, 258v–259v, and 267r.

entities.label: Lincoln Consistory Court Wills, Book 1520–31, current shelfmark unresolved
entities.description: The consistory-court will book containing the inventory of John Asserby printed as main group XXIII at ff. 59r, 59A, and 60r. The current Lincolnshire Archives fonds is identified, but no exact present shelfmark crosswalk for Clark's historical book label was established publicly.

holding.notes: Current exact request reference remains unresolved; use the PROBATE fonds record and archive enquiry route rather than treating PROBATE as an item shelfmark.

notes: Main XXIII is John Asserby's 1527 inventory at ff. 59r, 59A, and 60r.
A portion of the older EETS transcript was lost and replaced by Foster; the edition does not delimit the replaced span.

entities.label: Lincolnshire Archives LCC WILLS/1541
entities.description: The consistory-court will book now requested as LCC WILLS/1541, containing Richard Newcome's will printed as main group LX at Clark's ff. 164r–165v.

holding.notes: Current item-level request reference established by the Lincolnshire Archives catalog crosswalk.

notes: Main LX is Richard Newcome's will at Clark's ff. 164r–165v. Clark says it was one of dozens of similar wills in the set of consistory records.

The witness audit identified FL/TRANSCRIPTS/B/14, an abstract covering the 1541 book at ff. 147–282, and LCL/5306, a nineteenth-century copy, as later crosswalk corroboration. They are not modeled as textual sources, facsimiles, or access routes because Clark's source chain points to the original book, their full content and accessibility were not directly inspected, and neither is an actionable complete reproduction in this review.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Clark's 1914 Lincoln Diocese Documents
entities.description: A complete scan of a University of Florida copy of the 1914 EETS volume, exposed with PDF, OCR, scandata, and item metadata derivatives.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 410-page PDF scan with OCR and structural metadata derivatives
notes: Representative inspection confirms legible title and series pages, Forewords p. 1, encoded opening p. 37, encoded closing p. 270, and final edition p. 382.
The scan is predominantly grayscale and readable; OCR is useful for navigation but contains recognition errors and was not used as authority where page images were available.
Completeness is relative to the 1914 printed edition. This object is not a facsimile of any episcopal register or consistory book.

entities.label: Gale microfilm of Lincoln episcopal registers, reels 14–18
entities.description: Commercial microfilm listed in Gale's Church Authority and Power in Medieval and Early Modern England register series, covering the six episcopal-register source groups modeled here on reels 14–18. Exact physical-object surface coverage has not been audited from the reels.
entities.reproduction_format: Commercial microfilm; Lumley and Chedworth reel 14, Russell reel 15, Smith registers reel 16, Atwater and Longland reels 17–18
notes: The guide proves a commercial reproduction route and reel assignments, not anonymous public image access or complete coverage of covers, boards, endleaves, blanks, and every register surface.

entities.label: Langham Village History Group guide to Lincolnshire Archives register references
entities.description: A 2020 local-history research guide used as a supplemental crosswalk for current DIOC/REG references and Lincolnshire Archives MF shelfmarks. It is not an official archive catalog.

entities.label: Paul Ayris's 2026 diocesan-register reference guide in Thomas Cranmer's Register
entities.description: A current scholarly appendix identifying DIOC/REG/26–28 as containing parts of John Longland's and later bishops' registers; used to support the present DIOC shelfmark context without making it an upstream textual source.

bibliographic.citation: Paul Ayris, Thomas Cranmer's Register: A Record of Archiepiscopal Administration in Diocese and Province, Part I (London: UCL Press, 2026), introduction bibliography, p. 73.
bibliographic.title: Thomas Cranmer's Register: A Record of Archiepiscopal Administration in Diocese and Province

entities.label: Williamson appendix listing Lincoln episcopal-register ordinal designations
entities.description: A scholarly reference appendix listing the historical ordinal designations and episcopal coverage of the Lincoln registers, used only as supporting crosswalk evidence.

rights.component: Underlying documentary texts dated 1450–1544, distinct from modern photographs, transcriptions, markup, and editorial matter
rights.basis: The historical documentary texts predate modern copyright terms by several centuries. This statement does not govern modern images, archive services, transcripts, or contractual access conditions.

rights.component: Clark's 1914 edited text, introductions, notes, arrangement, indexes, typography, and other edition matter
rights.basis: The volume was published in 1914, and U-M currently presents the derived item as Public Domain / No Copyright–United States. Modern scan packaging and provider terms are separate.

rights.component: Materials presented in the current U-M CME item LinDDoc, including its transcription and markup
rights.basis: The current item and collection pages state that the materials are in the public domain in the United States and apply No Copyright–United States.
notes: This is a provider assertion limited to the United States, not a worldwide dedication and not a license for archive photographs or commercial microfilm.

rights.component: Pinned U-M-derived LinDDoc XML transcription and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The pinned XML permits personal, research, and teaching copies but forbids redistribution by commercial and not-for-profit information providers, while U-M's current item asserts Public Domain / No Copyright–United States. The relationship between those statements is unresolved.

rights.component: Repository software layer and any LinDDoc serialization or changes only to the extent that the CME Unlicense actually covers them
rights.basis: CME/LICENSE contains the Unlicense but explicitly describes its subject as software. It does not establish that upstream U-M transcription, markup, or embedded provider terms are superseded.

rights.component: University of Florida / Internet Archive page images, OCR, scandata, PDF packaging, and interface assets
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The derivatives are publicly downloadable, but the exact item metadata supplies no item-level reuse license for the modern reproduction layer. Public access is not treated as permission.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of DIOC/REG/19
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of DIOC/REG/20
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of DIOC/REG/22
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of the probable DIOC/REG/24 Smith register
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found. The volume crosswalk remains subject to archive confirmation.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of DIOC/REG/25
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of DIOC/REG/26
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for register images was found.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of the unresolved 1520–1531 LCC WILLS book
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; exact item identification must precede any order.

rights.component: Modern Lincolnshire Archives photographs, scans, or supplied reproductions of LCC WILLS/1541
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and archive service terms
rights.basis: Archive copying guidance separates personal-use photography, staff-supplied copies, and permission or fees for publication and reuse; no open license for the book's images was found.

rights.component: Gale microfilm images, reel packaging, and commercial reproduction layer
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The product guide establishes a commercial microfilm series but supplies no open reuse license. Reel access and reuse require enquiry to Gale or a holding institution.

rights.component: Paul Ayris's 2026 text and editorial matter, excluding separately credited third-party material
rights.basis: UCL Press publishes the book under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0, with third-party material subject to its own notices.

rights.component: Langham Village History Group research-guide PDF
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The guide is publicly downloadable, but no express open license was established during this review.

rights.component: Williamson appendix PDF and its scholarly register list
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The appendix is publicly downloadable through DIAMM, but no express open license was established during this review.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A much larger body of episcopal-register and consistory-court material
editorial_practices.representation: Sixty-three chronologically arranged English or quasi-English documentary groups selected under Furnivall, followed by thirteen additional groups supplied by Foster
editorial_practices.description: The edition constructs a seventy-six-documentary-group research selection across eight physical witness groups. Its Roman numbers are editorial group boundaries, not proof of seventy-six independent original instruments or complete register coverage.
editorial_practices.scope: Main I–LXIII and Appendix I–XIII, printed pp. 37–270

editorial_practices.source_feature: Register and consistory-book entries unavailable to Clark through his own inspection
editorial_practices.representation: Edited text derived from an unnamed copyist's Furnivall-era transcripts, Foster's supplied transcripts, and Foster's manuscript collation
editorial_practices.description: Clark explicitly says he never inspected the registers. The edition therefore preserves multiple material mediation layers and is not modeled as Clark's direct transcription from the physical witnesses.
editorial_practices.scope: Historical text of all seventy-six documentary groups

editorial_practices.source_feature: A multitude of doubtful places in the unnamed copyist's main-sequence transcripts
editorial_practices.representation: Foster checked passages against the manuscript sources and supplied corrections, archival information, and permanent folio references after refoliation
editorial_practices.description: The edition establishes broad manuscript collation but does not identify every checked reading or prove a line-by-line collation of every main group.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Formal Latin proceedings and repetitive legal or administrative formulae surrounding the selected vernacular matter
editorial_practices.representation: Clark summarizes or abbreviates some Latin procedural content in editorial English while printing selected Latin and vernacular text
editorial_practices.description: The printed groups are editions of selected record content, not diplomatic facsimiles of every word, layout feature, endorsement, or adjacent entry in the registers.
editorial_practices.scope: Documentary groups containing summarized or abbreviated formal matter

editorial_practices.source_feature: Clark's complete 1914 volume, including preliminaries, forewords, selected documents, grammar notes, additional notes, textual notes, and indexes
editorial_practices.representation: Printed title page and documentary sequence on pp. 37–270, including appendix introductions, with substantial apparatus omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text retains the sixty-three main groups and thirteen appendix groups but omits the Prefatory Note, main Forewords, contents, Grammar Notes, Additional Notes, textual notes, indexes, and most original layout information.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole U-M encoding and pinned repository artifact

evidence.citation: General Corpus / pinned Kyvero-Press CME source XML, LinDDoc.xml, commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Identifies LinDDoc, Clark's 1914 source edition, U-M production and selection declarations, legacy availability terms, sixty-three-plus-thirteen hierarchy, source notes, folio milestones, historical and editorial language evidence, and encoded text boundaries.

evidence.citation: Reproducible namespace-aware structural inspection of the pinned LinDDoc.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 2 DIV1, 76 DIV2, 156 DIV3, 1,485 P, 234 PB, 156 MILESTONE, and 2 L elements; the 63 main DIV2 contain 134 DIV3 and the 13 appendix DIV2 contain 22 DIV3. The two L elements are a Burns quotation in editorial commentary, so the substantive form is prose.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense but frames its subject as software; it does not expressly reconcile upstream U-M transcription and markup or the legacy availability notice embedded in LinDDoc.xml.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, LinDDoc item.
evidence.summary: Current item identity, public HTML text, Clark source citation, navigation for the main and appendix divisions, stable handle, and Public Domain / No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.summary: Describes the collection's manually keyed and proofread texts, public access and bulk XML context, and No Copyright–United States statement for collection materials.

evidence.citation: Current public raw XML download for LinDDoc.
evidence.summary: A complete and well-formed 535,291-byte current raw digital object with an ETS/EEBO wrapper and newer availability statement. It represents the same identified U-M item but is not byte-identical or structurally identical to the pinned repository XML.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Diocese Documents, 1450–1544, ed. Andrew Clark, EETS Original Series 149 (London, 1914), complete Internet Archive scan lincolndiocesedo00lond.
evidence.locator: title and series pages; Prefatory Note pp. v–vi; Forewords pp. 1–36; documents pp. 37–270; Grammar Notes pp. 271–341; Additional Notes pp. 342–344; indexes pp. 345–382
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the edition title, seventy-six-document selection boundary, contributors, Furnivall's selection and transcript history, Foster's collation and supplied copies, Clark's non-inspection of the registers, language and subject scope, complete apparatus, and printed source notes.

evidence.citation: Document-by-document inspection of Clark's printed source captions and internal folio marks against the complete 1914 scan.
evidence.locator: main I–LXIII and Appendix I–XIII source captions and folio marks, especially main XII, XXIII, XXXI, LVII, LX, LXIII, and Appendix IX
evidence.summary: Maps the selection to six episcopal-register groups and two consistory-book groups; confirms the transcript intermediaries; identifies Clark f. 238v rather than XML 283v for main XXXI, Clark f. 278r for main LVII's second milestone, and the main LXIII conflict between caption ff. 229v–230v and internal marks ff. 299v–300v. Appendix IX remains a high-confidence DIOC/REG/24 inference pending archive confirmation.

evidence.citation: Representative page and completeness inspection of the cached Clark 1914 PDF.
evidence.locator: scan title matter; printed pp. 1, 37, 270, and 382; first and final PDF pages
evidence.summary: Confirms a readable complete 410-page scan through printed p. 382, including the U-M-encoded selection and every omitted apparatus section. qpdf --check completed without warnings.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata, OCR, and scandata for item lincolndiocesedo00lond.
evidence.summary: Identifies the University of Florida source copy, digitization and derivative files. The exact metadata contains no item-level rights or license field for the scan layer; cached JSON and scandata parse cleanly, while OCR is navigational only.

evidence.citation: Lincolnshire Archives CalmView records for DIOC/REG/19, /20, /22–/26 and the LCC WILLS series crosswalk.
evidence.summary: Supports current holdings, dates, folio extents, composite-section boundaries, local microfilm references, the exact LCC WILLS/1541 mapping, and the high-confidence preference for DIOC/REG/24 over /23 for Appendix IX. DIOC/REG/26 has 310 folios and contains all Clark Longland loci; DIOC/REG/27's Longland portion ends too early.
notes: The LCC WILLS/1541 URL is a constructed current-ID route based on the established shelfmark; the catalog timed out during final live verification. Catalog record images were temporarily unavailable because of technical issues.
The audit also identified later derivatives FL/TRANSCRIPTS/B/14 and LCL/5306 as crosswalk corroboration only; neither was directly inspected or treated as an actionable complete surrogate.

evidence.citation: Lincolnshire Archives PROBATE fonds record.
evidence.summary: Identifies the probate and consistory-court record context and LCC WILLS series, but the parent fonds is not itself an item request reference and exposes no public item crosswalk for Clark's 1520–1531 book.

evidence.citation: Lincolnshire County Council, Lincolnshire Archives overview.
evidence.summary: Identifies Lincolnshire Archives as custodian of Lincoln Diocesan Record Office materials, including bishops' rolls and registers from 1214, and supplies the archive's contact context.

evidence.citation: Lincolnshire County Council, visiting and booking Lincolnshire Archives.
evidence.summary: Requires advance booking, full references, a reader ticket, and a bounded document order; supports the onsite access methods without claiming automatic production of originals.

evidence.citation: Lincolnshire County Council, Lincolnshire Archives research, photography, copying, and publication fees.
evidence.summary: Separates personal-use camera permission, staff-supplied copying, condition and page checks, publication or reuse permission, and applicable fees. It supplies archive.copies@lincolnshire.gov.uk as the reproduction contact.

evidence.citation: Langham Village History Group, Lincolnshire Archives source-reference guide, revised 11 November 2020.
evidence.summary: Supplemental, non-official crosswalk for DIOC/REG shelfmarks and Lincolnshire Archives MF 6 microfilm routes. The complete 720,006-byte guide passed qpdf --check without warnings.

evidence.citation: Gale, Church Authority and Power in Medieval and Early Modern England, Lincoln diocesan-register microfilm guide.
evidence.summary: Lists Lumley and Chedworth on reel 14, Russell on reel 15, both Smith registers on reel 16, and Atwater and Longland registers on reels 17–18. The complete guide passed qpdf --check; the actual reels were not inspected and current purchasability requires enquiry.

evidence.citation: Paul Ayris, Thomas Cranmer's Register (UCL Press, 2026), diocesan-register guide and appendix.
evidence.summary: Current scholarly support for the DIOC/REG/26–28 Longland and successor-register context and the CC BY-NC 4.0 publication terms. The complete 10,410,426-byte PDF opened and was inspectable; qpdf reported recoverable linearization and hint-table warnings.

evidence.citation: Williamson, appendix listing Lincoln episcopal registers, DIAMM public PDF.
evidence.summary: Supplemental historical register-sequence evidence used only where consistent with current shelfmarks and catalogs. The complete 2,360,453-byte PDF opened and was inspectable; qpdf reported recoverable linearization and hint-table warnings.

evidence.citation: Dated search for publicly accessible complete facsimiles of the six episcopal-register and two consistory-book source groups.
evidence.summary: No anonymous public complete-register or complete-consistory-book image package was found on 2026-07-15 after checking the official catalog and archive site, HathiTrust, Google Books, Internet Archive, FamilySearch, commercial microfilm guides, and general scholarly search. Archive onsite, copying, and commercial microfilm routes remain and the negative finding is date-bound.

open_questions.question: Can Lincolnshire Archives confirm that Appendix IX f. 87r–v is DIOC/REG/24 rather than DIOC/REG/23?
next_steps: Ask archive staff to crosswalk Clark's William Smith f. 87 commission to the present shelfmark.
Inspect both candidate folio 87 openings if needed; retain DIOC/REG/24 as a high-confidence inference rather than a confirmed identifier until then.

open_questions.question: What is the current item-level LCC WILLS request reference for Clark's 'Lincoln Consistory Court Wills, Book 1520–31,' ff. 59, 59A, and 60?
next_steps: Send the historical book label, John Asserby inventory date 1527, and Clark folio sequence to Lincolnshire Archives.
Do not use the PROBATE parent record as an item shelfmark or substitute a later transcript.

open_questions.question: Can direct inspection of DIOC/REG/26 resolve the inherited Longland folio conflicts for main XXXI, LVII, and especially LXIII?
next_steps: Inspect main XXXI at f. 238v and main LVII's second locus at f. 278r, comparing the pinned XML transpositions with Clark's printed folio marks.
Inspect both ff. 229v–230v and 299v–300v for main LXIII; current internal folio marks and chronology strongly support ff. 299v–300v, but preserve the caption conflict.
Check surviving Furnivall transcripts or Foster refoliation notes if located.

open_questions.question: Which side of DIOC/REG/20 f. 14 contains main XII, and how much of the underlying record did the edition omit?
next_steps: Inspect f. 14r and f. 14v with neighboring leaves and compare Clark's statement that the text is presumably incomplete.
Keep physical record extent separate from the printed documentary-group boundary.

open_questions.question: Do the Furnivall-era transcript bundle, Foster's main-sequence collation, and Foster's supplied appendix transcripts survive, and where?
next_steps: Search EETS and Oxford archival records, Clark and Furnivall papers, and Foster's papers for the working copies and correspondence.
If found, distinguish the unnamed copyist's transcript, Foster's corrections, the thirteen supplied copies, and the replacement span for main XXIII.

open_questions.question: Can complete reusable image objects for all six episcopal registers and both consistory books be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Ask Lincolnshire Archives for existing or newly created complete-object photography, separately from the selected leaves, with exact coverage and reuse terms.
Ask Gale or holding libraries whether reels 14–18 remain available and audit actual first and last filmed surfaces before describing them as complete.
Cache only permitted deliverables and record full-object versus work-portion coverage explicitly.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current Public Domain / No Copyright–United States statement supersede the pinned XML notice that restricts redistribution by commercial and not-for-profit information providers?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing the pinned transcription or substantially copied markup.
Ask Kyvero Press whether CME's software-worded Unlicense was intended to cover upstream XML data and how embedded provider terms are handled.
Keep underlying historical text, Clark's edition, U-M transcription and markup, repository changes, and scan or witness images as separate rights layers.

open_questions.question: What transformation and revision history connects the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS LinDDoc.xml to U-M's current ETS/EEBO-wrapped raw XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M for production provenance and revision records for both serializations.
Compare content, structure, identifiers, availability statements, and omitted or added markup without calling the two files byte-identical.
